title: Crane drivers
description: Perform a variety of attending duties at amusement or recreation facility. May schedule use of recreation facilities, maintain and provide equipment to participants of sporting events or recreational pursuits, or operate amusement concessions and rides. Operate or tend machinery at surface mining site, equipped with scoops, shovels, or buckets to excavate and load loose materials. Operate a variety of drills such as rotary, churn, and pneumatic to tap subsurface water and salt deposits, to remove core samples during mineral exploration or soil testing, and to facilitate the use of explosives in mining or construction. Includes horizontal and earth boring machine operators. (Based on 17 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Drill holes into roofs at specified distances from ribs or adjacent bolts, conduct safety checks on equipment, and check the stability of roof and rib support systems before mining face areas.
Pull down loose rock that cannot be supported and scrape or wash conveyors to minimize dust production.
Conduct methane gas checks to ensure breathing quality of air and perform ventilation tasks, such as hanging ventilation tubing and curtains.
Insert wedges and feathers into holes and drive wedges with sledgehammers to split stone sections from masses; remove pieces of stone from larger masses using tools.
Operate mining machines to gather coal and convey it to floors or shuttle cars; drive machines into position at working faces.
Perform routine maintenance and upgrade work on machines and equipment, such as replacing parts and lubricating machinery; repair, oil, and adjust machines.
Control conveyors that run the length of shuttle cars to distribute loads; move controls to start and regulate movement of conveyors and to start and position drill cutters or torches.
Signal workers to ensure the safe movement of loaded cars and help coordinate the laying of track and resetting supports and blocking.

Connect electrical wire to primers and verify detonation of charges by observing control panels or listening for the sounds of blasts; light fuses and drop detonating devices into wells or boreholes to detonate explosives.
Assemble and position machines and equipment for drilling operations, select and attach drill bits, and start, stop, and control drilling speed of machines.
Examine blast areas to ensure safety laws are observed, measure depths of drilled blast holes and mark patterns for drilling to issue drilling instructions.
Monitor loading processes to ensure materials are loaded according to specifications and observe hand signals or other markings when operating machines.
Drive or guide truck-mounted equipment into position, level and stabilize rigs, and operate controls to flush earth cuttings or to blow dust from holes.
Maintain inventory levels and order new supplies as necessary; compile and keep gun and explosives records in compliance with laws.
Drive trucks to transport explosives and blasting equipment to blasting sites; handle high voltage sources and hang electrical cables.
Remove drill bits from chucks after drilling holes, and insert bolts into chucks; rotate chucks to turn bolts and open expansion heads against rock formations.
Perform inspections of core samples, document geological formations encountered during work, and retrieve lost equipment from bore holes.
Direct other workers engaged in placing blocks or outriggers to prevent capsizing of machines; clean and lubricate equipment while making minor repairs as necessary.
Observe equipment gauges and indicators, hand signals of other workers, and other markings to verify load positions or depths and ensure work is performed to specifications.
Start engines of hoists or winches and use levers and pedals to wind or unwind cable, moving levers, pedals, and throttles to regulate speeds of hoist or winch drums in response to signals.
Direct movements of vessels in locks or bridge areas, using signals and telecommunications equipment.
Clean, lubricate, and maintain mechanisms such as cables, pulleys, or grappling devices, making repairs as necessary and inspecting for wear and damage.
Load or unload bundles from trucks, move containers to storage bins using moving equipment, and move materials over short distances around a construction site or warehouse.
Direct helpers engaged in placing blocking or outrigging under cranes, and drive vehicles or operate machines to assist with work assignments.
Inspect and adjust crane mechanisms or lifting accessories, and record details of attendance, sales, receipts, reservations, or repair activities.
Determine load weights and check them against lifting capacities to prevent overload, and set up or inspect equipment prior to operation.
Become familiar with digging plans, machine capabilities and limitations, and ensure adherence to safety rules and regulations.
Receive written or oral instructions regarding material movement or excavation, and review work or delivery schedules to determine sequences or special instructions.
Monitor activities to ensure adherence to rules and safety procedures.
Provide information about facilities, entertainment options, and rules and regulations to patrons, and direct patrons to rides, seats, or attractions.
Explain and demonstrate work tasks to new workers, assign training tasks, and confer with supervisors or other personnel to resolve problems.
Plan work assignments and equipment allocations to meet operational goals and enforce safety rules and regulations.
Inspect or test materials, vehicles, equipment, or facilities to ensure safety and conformity with specifications.
Dispatch personnel and vehicles in response to emergencies and requisition necessary supplies or repair services.
Monitor field work to ensure proper performance and verify records of time, materials, or crew activities.
Operate machinery to perform activities such as backfilling excavations or breaking rock or concrete, and lubricate or repair machinery or replace parts as necessary.
Inspect bundle packaging for conformance to regulations or customer requirements.
Weigh bundles using floor scales and record weights for company records.
Schedule the use of recreation facilities, such as golf courses and bowling alleys, and provide assistance to patrons entering or exiting rides or vehicles.
Drive machines to work sites and adjust dig face angles for varying overburden depths.
Sell tickets and collect fees from customers, and verify or collect tickets before admitting patrons to venues.
Rent or sell sporting equipment and supplies to customers.
Maintain inventories of equipment, storing and retrieving items, and cleaning facilities or grounds as needed.
Announce or describe amusement park attractions to entice customers to games or entertainment.
Inspect work areas or equipment for cleanliness or adherence to established standards.
Meet with managers or supervisors to stay informed of changes affecting operations.
Participate in continuing education to stay abreast of industry trends.
Apply customer feedback to service improvement efforts and train workers in operational procedures.
Take disciplinary action regarding worker performance.

emerging_tasks.task: Check roof or ribs for hazardous conditions.
Clean equipment, such as dust collectors.
Operate drones for aerial survey of blast sites and for post-blast damage assessment.
Inspect crane site conditions to determine ground stability.
